content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Gary Frago 
1899 Third street 
Atwater, CA 
95301 
Dear Mr. Frago: 
March 7, 1986 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. 1-86-07l 
This is in response to your letter, dated February 18, 
1986, requesting advice on legality of holding elective office 
in the City of Atwater while being employed by the City. 
The Political Reform Act does not address this problem; 
however, the Attorney General has addressed this issue. 
Enclosed is a list of Attorney General opinions on this 
subject. 
These opinions should be available at your county law 
library. 
As an alternative, you may wish to directly contact the 
Attorney General's Office for specific advice regarding your 
situation. 
I hope that this information is helpful to you. 
REL:JG:plh 
Enclosure 
Sincerely, 
~/I 
! _L.)-.~ 
.. 
7'll.-<-
. 
/ 
W 
/1'; 
~) 
Robert E. Leidigh /1 
Counsel 
Legal Division 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916)322~5660 


INCOMPATIBILITY OF PUBLIC OFFICES 
The FPPC receives numerous requests for information 
regarding the propriety of simultaneously holding two public 
offices with overlapping jurisdictions. 
The Political Reform 
Act does not deal with this subject: however, the Attorney 
General has issued numerous published opinions on 
incompatibility of offices. 
The following is a partial list of 
recent opinions. 
Although it is not an exhaustive list of the 
opinions on this subject, it should provide a starting point for 
research. 
68 Ope Att. Gen. 
7 (1984) 
67 Ope Att. Gen. 409 (1984) 
66 Ope Att. Gen. 382 (1983) 
66 Ope Att. Gen. 293 (1983) 
66 Ope Att. Gen. 176 (1983) 
65 Ope Att. Gen. 606 (1982) 
64 Ope Att. Gen. 795 (1981) 
64 Ope Att. Gen. 288 (1981) 
64 Ope Att. Gen. 137 (1981) 


February 18, 1986 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 IfJ" Street, Suite 800 
Sacramento, Ca 95814 
Dear Sir or Madam: 
I am anticipating running for a seat on the Atwater City 
Council this year. 
Since I am an employee of the City of Atwater, I was recently 
advised that I would be unable to hold an elective or appointed 
office in the same city. 
Could you be so kind as to send me any information regarding 
this question, or if possible, a reading from one of your 
attorneys with the Fair Political Practices Commission. 
I would be most appreciative. 
Yours very truly, 
1899 Third Street 
Atwater. Ca 95301 
209-358-6252 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Gary Frago 
1899 Third street 
Atwater, CA 
95301 
Dt::'ar Mr. Frago: 
February 26, 1986 
Re: 
I-86-071 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform 
Act has been received by the Fair Political Practices 
Commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice 
request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5901. 
We try ,to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, 
or unless more information is needed to answer your request, 
you should expect a response within 21 working days. 
REL:plh 
Very truly yours, 
--/

Robert E. Leidi'ghi v', 
Counsel

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • 
Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~5660

sections.question: Could you be so kind as to send me any information regarding this question, or if possible, a reading from one of your attorneys with the Fair Political Practices Commission. I would be most appreciative. [Context: Whether I would be unable to hold an elective or appointed office in the same city where I am employed as a City employee, specifically regarding running for Atwater City Council while being an employee of the City of Atwater.]
sections.conclusion: The Political Reform Act does not address this problem; however, the Attorney General has addressed this issue. Enclosed is a list of Attorney General opinions on this subject. These opinions should be available at your county law library. As an alternative, you may wish to directly contact the Attorney General's Office for specific advice regarding your situation.
sections.question_synthetic: Can a person who is employed by a city hold an elective office in the same city, specifically whether an employee of the City of Atwater can run for and serve on the Atwater City Council?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC determined that the Political Reform Act does not directly address the incompatibility of offices issue, but referred the requester to published Attorney General opinions on the subject and recommended contacting the Attorney General's Office for specific advice.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request letter (February 18, 1986) and the FPPC responses (February 26 and March 7, 1986 letters). The March 7 letter is the primary substantive response. No formal CONCLUSION section header exists; conclusion text extracted from body of March 7 response letter. Enclosure listing Attorney General opinions provided. High confidence in extraction due to clear correspondence structure.

QUESTION: Could you be so kind as to send me any information regarding this question, or if possible, a reading from one of your attorneys with the Fair Political Practices Commission. I would be most appreciative. [Context: Whether I would be unable to hold an elective or appointed office in the same city where I am employed as a City employee, specifically regarding running for Atwater City Council while being an employee of the City of Atwater.]

CONCLUSION: The Political Reform Act does not address this problem; however, the Attorney General has addressed this issue. Enclosed is a list of Attorney General opinions on this subject. These opinions should be available at your county law library. As an alternative, you may wish to directly contact the Attorney General's Office for specific advice regarding your situation.

embedding.summary: Gary Frago requested FPPC advice on whether he could hold an elective office on the Atwater City Council while employed by the City of Atwater. The FPPC responded that while the Political Reform Act does not address this issue, the Attorney General has issued numerous opinions on incompatibility of offices and provided a list of relevant AG opinions for the requester's review.
